parse.title: Mining
parse.text:
Mining is the skill associated with breaking rocks, mainly in the Mines. Larger or more valuable rocks and nodes take more hits, and in some cases, require a stronger Pickaxe to break.

Contents

  • 1 Mines Access
  • 2 Mining Skill
  • 3 Experience Points
  • 4 Mining Nodes
  • 5 Food
  • 6 The Mines
  • 7 Skull Cavern
  • 8 The Quarry
  • 9 Volcano Dungeon
  • 10 References
  • 11 History

Mines Access[edit]

During the first four days of the game, the Mines are closed due to a landslide caused by the Joja Corporation. On Spring 5 in the first year, a letter will be received in the mail saying that Joja Corp. has cleared the landslide, and then the Mines are unlocked.

Mining Skill[edit]

Mining Skill is increased by breaking rocks, panning, or by reading Mining Monthly or the Book Of Stars. Each level adds +1 to Pickaxe proficiency.

Mining skill points are awarded when rocks are destroyed — it doesn't matter if this is done by Pickaxe or Bombs. Rocks destroyed by Monsters grant no XP.

Level 1 Level 2 Level 3 Level 4 Level 5

Experience Points[edit]

250 Mining XP is gained for reading a copy of Mining Monthly or the Book Of Stars. One XP is given for each piece of ore gained from panning (applies to Copper, Iron, Gold, or Iridium ore).[1]

Different rock types give different numbers of experience points. While generic rocks inside the Mines provide no experience bonus on their own, if coal is dropped, 5 experience points are awarded (except in the case of the two gray rocks pictured below). No other "extra" drops from breaking rocks inside the Mines award experience points.

Breaking open a meteor dropped on the Farm during a Meteorite event awards no experience.

Experience level is increased immediately upon breaking a rock/boulder/node, but the "level up" window doesn't appear until after going to sleep.

The Geologist profession gives the specific gem nodes a 50% chance of spawning twice the gems. If this happens, then the amount of experience gained for each node is reduced, this is labelled as "Multiple Spawns" in the table below.[2] Note that the mining mastery results in the number of gems always being doubled, but does not reduce the experience gained.

Rock Type Experience
Any Rock (outside the Mines) 1
If coal is dropped: 1 or 6[3]
Boulder 0
Rock, except for the rocks below (inside the Mines and the Volcano Dungeon) 0
If coal/ore dropped: 5
Either of these dark gray rocks (inside the Mines)
Any of these 3 rocks (inside the Mines when dangerous and inside the Volcano Dungeon) 		3
If coal is dropped: 4
Amethyst Node 16

Aquamarine Node 40
Multiple spawns: 20
Diamond Node 150

Gem Node Dependent on received gem (see other gem nodes)

Mining Nodes[edit]

Quarry

Volcano Dungeon

Skull Cavern

Mystic Stone 1-3 Iridium Ore, 1-4 Gold Ore, and a 25% chance of 1 Prismatic Shard Floor 100+ Skull Cavern, Quarry, Volcano Dungeon
Ruby Node Ruby Any floor after reaching the bottom of the Mines; floor 80+ otherwise Skull Cavern, Quarry, Volcano Dungeon
Topaz Node Topaz Any floor Skull Cavern, Quarry
Geode Node Geode N/A Hill-top and Four Corners farm maps
Frozen Geode Node Frozen Geode N/A Hill-top and Four Corners farm maps after Mining Skill level 5[4]
Magma Geode Node Magma Geode N/A Hill-top and Four Corners farm maps after Mining Skill level 8[4]
Bone Node Bone Fragments, and a chance for Fossilized Leg, Fossilized Ribs, Prehistoric Scapula, Prehistoric Tibia, Prehistoric Skull, Skeletal Hand, Prehistoric Rib, Prehistoric Vertebra, Skeletal Tail, Nautilus Fossil, Amphibian Fossil, Palm Fossil or Trilobite[2] N/A Ginger Island Dig Site
Cinder Shard Node Cinder Shards N/A Volcano Dungeon
Clay Node Clay N/A Ginger Island Dig Site
Omni Geode Node Omni Geode N/A Volcano Dungeon
Radioactive Node Radioactive Ore Any floor when the Mines are in a "Dangerous" state. Cannot spawn on floors that are a multiple of 5. Dangerous Skull Cavern
Mussel Node Mussels N/A Ginger Island West
Calico Egg Node Calico Egg N/A Skull Cavern while the Desert Festival is going on
Coal Node Coal N/A Quarry, Quarry Mine, Volcano Dungeon

Diamond Nodes begin appearing at floor 50 in the Mines, at approximately .0020 chance (1 in 500) of appearing in place of a stone. For every floor after floor 50, frequency increases by .000016 per floor. This makes for a .0028 chance for Diamond Node to appear in place of a stone at floor 100 (1.4 in 500 or 7 in 2500), and ultimately a .003104 chance for a diamond node to appear in place of a stone at floor 119 (1.552 in 500 or 194 in 62500).

The chance for Gem Nodes to appear is very small, but is slightly increased by Mining Skill level and daily luck. The chance for Mystic Stones to appear is also increased by Mining Skill Level and daily luck.

Food[edit]

Certain cooked dishes will temporarily increase the player's mining level. Qi Seasoning can be applied to further increase the stat buff for dishes cooked by the player.

References[edit]

  1. ↑ See Pan::getPanItems in the game code.
  2. ↑ 2.0 2.1 See GameLocation::breakStone in the game code.
  3. ↑ There are 2 different code sections to assign coal to a rock outside the Mines. One is breakStone, a chance of 5% * (1 + DailyLuck/2 + 0.005*MiningSkill) for a rock giving coal which does give the +5 bonus. The other is onStoneDestroyed, a 3.5% chance for a coal that doesn't give bonus XP. From a player's view, the game will randomly give 6 XP sometimes for coal-dropping rocks, and sometimes just 1 randomly. Prospector causes breakStone to roll twice for the coal drop (but the extra roll grants no XP), while doubling the chances for onStoneDestroyed to 7%.
  4. ↑ 4.0 4.1 See Farm::doDailyMountainFarmUpdate in the game code.

History[edit]

  • 1.0: Introduced.
  • 1.1: The Blacksmith profession now increases the value of metal bars by 50%, up from 25%.
  • 1.4: Fixed Prospector profession not applied to rocks destroyed in the Mines.
  • 1.5: Added Bone Node, Cinder Shard Node, Clay Node, Omni Geode Node, Radioactive Node, and Mussel Node.
  • 1.5.5: The Blacksmith profession now affects Radioactive Bars.
  • 1.6: Reduced chance for the Iridium Node to drop a Prismatic Shard from 4% to 3.5%. Changed Volcano Dungeon Gold Node sprite. Added Calico Egg Node and Coal Node. Rocks destroyed by monsters no longer give Mining XP. Mining Experience can now be gained by panning, reading Mining Monthly or the Book Of Stars.

Skills Farming • Mining • Foraging • Fishing • Combat
Stats Attack • Crit. Chance • Crit. Power • Defense • Immunity • Luck • Magnetism • Speed • Weight

'''Mining''' is the [[Skills|skill]] associated with breaking rocks, mainly in [[the Mines]]. Larger or more valuable rocks and nodes take more hits, and in some cases, require a stronger [[Pickaxes|Pickaxe]] to break. ==Mines Access== During the first four days of the game, [[the Mines]] are closed due to a landslide caused by the [[JojaMart|Joja Corporation]]. On [[Spring]] 5 in the first year, a letter will be received in the mail saying that Joja Corp. has cleared the landslide, and then the Mines are unlocked. ==[[File:Mining Skill Icon.png|32px]] Mining Skill== Mining Skill is increased by breaking rocks, [[Pans|panning]], or by reading [[Mining Monthly]] or the [[Book Of Stars]]. Each level adds +1 to [[Pickaxes|Pickaxe]] [[Skills#Proficiency|proficiency]]. Mining skill points are awarded when rocks are destroyed — it doesn't matter if this is done by [[Pickaxes|Pickaxe]] or [[Crafting#Bombs|Bombs]]. Rocks destroyed by [[Monsters]] grant no XP. {{:Mining/Skill}} ==Experience Points== 250 Mining XP is gained for reading a copy of [[Mining Monthly]] or the [[Book Of Stars]]. One XP is given for each piece of ore gained from [[Pans|panning]] (applies to [[Copper Ore|Copper]], [[Iron Ore|Iron]], [[Gold Ore|Gold]], or [[Iridium Ore|Iridium]] ore). Different rock types give different numbers of experience points. While generic rocks inside [[the Mines]] provide no experience bonus on their own, if [[coal]] is dropped, 5 experience points are awarded (except in the case of the two gray rocks pictured below). No other "extra" drops from breaking rocks inside the Mines award experience points. Breaking open a meteor dropped on [[the Farm]] during a [[Random Events#Meteorite|Meteorite event]] awards no experience. Experience level is increased immediately upon breaking a rock/boulder/node, but the "level up" window doesn't appear until after going to sleep. The Geologist profession gives the specific gem nodes a 50% chance of spawning twice the gems. If this happens, then the amount of experience gained for each node is reduced, this is labelled as "Multiple Spawns" in the table below. See Farm::doDailyMountainFarmUpdate in the game code. ==History== {{History|1.0|Introduced.}} {{History|1.1|The [[#Mining Skill|Blacksmith profession]] now increases the value of metal bars by 50%, up from 25%.}} {{History|1.4|Fixed Prospector profession not applied to rocks destroyed in [[the Mines]].}} {{History|1.5|Added [[#Bone Node|Bone Node]], [[#Cinder Shard Node|Cinder Shard Node]], [[#Clay Node|Clay Node]], [[#Omni Geode Node|Omni Geode Node]], [[#Radioactive Node|Radioactive Node]], and [[#Mussel Node|Mussel Node]].}} {{History|1.5.5|The Blacksmith profession now affects [[Radioactive Bar]]s.}} {{History|1.6|Reduced chance for the [[#Iridium Node|Iridium Node]] to drop a [[Prismatic Shard]] from 4% to 3.5%. Changed [[Volcano Dungeon]] [[#Gold Node|Gold Node]] sprite. Added [[#Calico Egg Node|Calico Egg Node]] and [[#Coal Node|Coal Node]]. Rocks destroyed by [[monsters]] no longer give Mining XP. Mining Experience can now be gained by [[Pans|panning]], reading [[Mining Monthly]] or the [[Book Of Stars]].}} {{NavboxSkillsStats}} [[Category:Mining]] [[de:Minenarbeit]] [[es:Minería]] [[fr:Extraction minière]] [[it:Estrazione]] [[ja:採鉱]] [[ko:채광]] [[hu:Bányászás]] [[pt:Mineração]] [[ru:Горное дело]] [[tr:Madencilik]] [[zh:采矿]]
